fields.category_description: Orchard and soft fruits picked at peak ripeness
Milk, cheese, butter and free-range eggs
Local honeys, jams, chutneys and preserves
Pasture-raised meat and poultry from local farms

fields.product_description: Rocket, spinach and baby lettuce harvested fresh each morning.
Purple, yellow and orange heritage varieties — sweeter than standard carrots.
Tender green courgettes, great for grilling or roasting.
Waxy Jersey-style new potatoes freshly dug. Perfect boiled with butter and mint.
Fat, flavourful leeks harvested throughout winter and spring.
Large, golden butternut squash with dense, sweet flesh. Stores well.
Fragrant sweet basil grown in polytunnels. Sold as a generous cut bunch.
Bright, vibrant flat-leaf parsley. A kitchen staple.
Classic tart Bramley apples, ideal for pies, crumbles and sauces.
Sweet, juicy Conference pears picked at perfect ripeness.
Sun-ripened strawberries bursting with flavour, picked to order.
Wild-hedgerow blackberries hand-picked from the orchard borders.
Tangy forced rhubarb with bright pink stalks. Great for crumbles and jams.
Full-fat unhomogenised milk from grass-fed Friesians. Cream rises to the top.
Light and fresh semi-skimmed milk from our Aberystwyth herd.
Large free-range eggs from hens that roam our pastures daily. Rich orange yolks.
Churned from fresh cream with a pinch of sea salt. Rich and spreadable.
Thick, creamy live natural yoghurt. No added sugar or thickeners.
Handmade 12-month aged cheddar. Crumbly texture with a sharp, nutty flavour.
Bread
Free-range eggs from our happy hens.
Rich, creamy duck eggs — great for baking.
Soft, tangy goat's cheese made on-site.
Pasture-raised, slow-grown chicken. Delivered fresh.
Tender young spinach leaves picked twice weekly.
Packed with flavour and nutrients. Great stir-fried.
Deep red, earthy beetroot. Roast whole or grate raw.
Crisp and peppery — perfect for salads.
Long-fermented sourdough with a crispy crust.
Dense, seeded wholemeal baked fresh each morning.
Unfiltered honey from our hives on the Mendip Hills.
Raw, unfiltered ACV with the mother. Unpasteurised.
Milk

fields.title: Our First Polytunnel Harvest
fields.description: After two years of planning we finally harvested our first full crop from the new polytunnels this week — basil, tomatoes and salad leaves all looking incredible. We can now supply fresh greens year-round. A huge thank you to everyone who helped with the build last autumn!

fields.title: Spring Update from the Orchard
fields.description: Blossom is out across all the apple and pear trees at Ceridwen's Orchard. If the weather holds we're expecting a bumper crop this autumn. Strawberry plants are in flower too — fingers crossed for a warm June!

fields.title: Heritage Carrot & Ginger Soup
fields.description:
Ingredients: 500 g Green Valley heritage carrots, 1 onion, 2 cloves garlic, thumb of fresh ginger, 1 L vegetable stock, 200 ml Sunny Meadow whole milk, salt & pepper.

Sweat onion, garlic and ginger in oil for 5 min. Add carrots and stock, simmer 20 min until tender. Blitz smooth, stir in the milk and season. Serves 4.

fields.title: Courgette, Herb & Egg Frittata
fields.description:
Ingredients: 3 courgettes (Green Valley), 6 eggs (Sunny Meadow), handful of fresh basil and parsley (Green Valley), 50 g mature cheddar (Sunny Meadow), salt & pepper.

Slice and fry courgettes until golden. Beat eggs, stir in herbs and cheese. Pour over courgettes in an ovenproof pan. Cook on hob 3 min then finish under a hot grill for 5 min. Serves 3–4.

fields.title: Winter Cover Crop Update (Internal)
fields.description: Overwinter rye and vetch across fields 3 and 4 looking healthy. Ploughing back in late April ahead of the brassica beds. Internal record only.

fields.title: Meet the Bees Behind Our Honey
fields.description: JakeProducer runs 12 hives across the Mendip Hills. This spring we extracted our first wildflower crop and the results are incredible — deep amber with notes of clover and heather. All honey is raw, cold-extracted and never heated above 40°C. Come and see the hives at our open day on 15th May!

fields.title: Roasted Beetroot & Goat's Cheese Tart
fields.description:
Ingredients: 4 Hillside Farm beetroot, 200g Alex Farms goat's cheese, 1 sheet shortcrust pastry, 2 tbsp balsamic glaze, handful of Green Valley rocket, salt & pepper.

Roast beetroot whole at 200°C for 45 min. Cool, peel and slice. Line a tart tin with pastry, blind bake 15 min. Layer beetroot, dot with goat's cheese, drizzle balsamic. Bake 20 min. Serve with rocket. Serves 4.

fields.title: Hillside Farm's Organic Journey
fields.description: We completed our 3-year organic conversion in March and are now fully certified. That means no synthetic fertilisers or pesticides on any of our fields. Our spinach, kale and beetroot are now verified organic — look for the Soil Association logo on our packaging. It's been a challenging transition but the soil health improvements are already visible.

fields.title: Cold Storage Upgrade — Internal Update
fields.description: We have installed two new 4°C cold rooms at the distribution hub. From next week all leafy greens will be stored at the hub rather than collected directly from farms. Producers: please ensure deliveries arrive by 7am on collection days. This is an internal update — not for public display.

fields.title: Sourdough French Toast with Wildflower Honey
fields.description:
Ingredients: 4 thick slices Jake's sourdough, 3 Sunny Meadow free-range eggs, 100ml whole milk, 1 tsp vanilla, 2 tbsp butter, 2 tbsp JakeProducer wildflower honey, fresh strawberries.

Whisk eggs, milk and vanilla. Dip bread slices and fry in butter 2-3 min each side until golden. Drizzle with honey and serve with sliced strawberries. Perfect weekend breakfast.

fields.title: BRFN Spring Market — Save the Date
fields.description: Our Spring Market returns to Aberystwyth town centre on Saturday 23rd May, 9am–2pm. All BRFN producers are invited to have a stall — contact the hub to register. Last year we had over 400 visitors and sold out of most produce by midday. This year we're expanding to the lower square to accommodate more producers.

fields.title: Alex Farms Welcomes a New Flock
fields.description: We've just taken delivery of 200 new point-of-lay hens — a mix of Leghorn and Rhode Island Red. They'll be producing eggs within the next 2-3 weeks, which means we can increase our weekly egg allocation to marketplace customers. Duck egg numbers are also up this season.

fields.title: Kale & Potato Soup
fields.description:
Ingredients: 1 bunch Hillside kale, 500g Green Valley new potatoes, 1 onion, 2 cloves garlic, 1L vegetable stock, 200ml Sunny Meadow whole milk, salt & pepper.

Sweat onion and garlic. Add diced potatoes and stock, simmer 15 min. Add roughly chopped kale and cook 5 more minutes. Stir in milk, season and serve. Hearty and simple. Serves 4.
